steps.title: Identify the tense established in the passage
steps.content: "In this chapter, we will focus on..." uses simple future tense. The next sentence describes a related action that will happen within the same chapter, so it must stay in the future tense for consistency.

steps.title: Apply verb tense consistency — per-choice diagnosis
steps.content:
Both actions belong to the same planned, future activity, so the verb in the blank must be future tense.
  • Choice A — "will have turned": future perfect. Implies the turning will be fully completed before some other future point — an unwarranted distinction not signaled in the passage. Eliminated.
  • Choice B — "turned": simple past. Contradicts the future context set by "we will focus." Eliminated.
  • Choice C — "had turned": past perfect. Describes a past action completed before another past event — completely at odds with the future framing. Eliminated.
  • Choice D — "will turn": simple future. Matches "will focus" and correctly places both actions in the same future sequence. Correct.
